what: Reconcile-on-write truth-maintenance on HaluMem is_update GT (n=60): the evidence-gate cost vs the NLI-judge lever. 100% local, deterministic, no claude -p.

local_nli_thr0.9_tiered_vs_strict_iter3.conclusion: TIERED preserves full update-recall (= none, 0.2833) AND protects evidenced facts (unit-tested); STRICT collapses to 0 recall. Tiered is the write-path default when auto-supersede is on.

findings: BOTTLENECK is conflict DETECTION, not the supersede decision: lexical max_diff=1 supersedes only 1.67% of real HaluMem updates (they are paraphrases, not 1-token edits).
The local NLI judge is the Updating lever: ~17x update-recall (0.0167 -> 0.2833 at thr=0.9) over lexical.
The anti-sycophancy evidence gate costs ~nothing on Updating here (removes 1/60): it is a near-free SAFETY property, not a recall regression.

OPEN precision problem: the NLI judge wrongly supersedes 6.7-10% of COMPLEMENTARY (same-entity, different-attribute) pairs. That deletes truth from the live set. This is the real barrier to shipping auto-supersede at high recall.

RESOLVED (iter3): the TIERED gate — require evidence ONLY to overwrite an EVIDENCED fact, allow bare->bare updates — preserves full update-recall (0.2833 = ungated) while keeping verified truth sycophancy-proof (unit-tested). Strict collapses recall to 0. Tiered is now the write-path default when auto-supersede is on.
